strategies.name: Govern (GV)
strategies.description: Establish and monitor the organization's cybersecurity risk management strategy, expectations, and policy. Covers organizational context, risk management strategy, roles and responsibilities, policy, oversight, and cybersecurity supply chain risk management.

ML1.summary: Organizational context and basic risk management strategy are established. Foundational governance structures define cybersecurity roles and document the operating environment.

ML2.summary: Risk management policies are formalized and communicated across the organization. Conditional access and compliance policies enforce governance requirements consistently.

ML3.summary: Governance processes are integrated into enterprise risk management. Policy enforcement is automated, continuously monitored, and reviewed for effectiveness.

strategies.name: Identify (ID)
strategies.description: Understand the organization's current cybersecurity risks. Covers asset management, risk assessment, and continuous improvement of cybersecurity posture.

ML1.summary: Hardware and software assets are inventoried and managed. Baseline compliance policies ensure devices meet minimum security configuration standards.

ML2.summary: Vulnerability assessments identify and prioritize risks. Software update compliance and patch status are tracked across endpoints to reduce the attack surface.

ML3.summary: Advanced asset discovery and risk assessment processes are in place. Device health attestation and security baselines are continuously validated and improved upon.

strategies.name: Protect (PR)
strategies.description: Use safeguards to prevent or reduce cybersecurity risk. Covers identity management and access control, awareness and training, data security, platform security, and technology infrastructure resilience.

ML1.summary: Foundational access controls and identity protections are implemented. MFA is enforced, legacy authentication is blocked, and basic data security policies restrict unauthorized access.

ML2.summary: Conditional access policies enforce risk-based and compliant-device requirements. Advanced data protection, anti-phishing, and Safe Attachments safeguards are deployed across workloads.

ML3.summary: Comprehensive platform hardening, network segmentation, and infrastructure resilience controls are enforced. Advanced threat protection, application restrictions, and Teams/SharePoint security hardening are fully deployed.

strategies.name: Detect (DE)
strategies.description: Find and analyze possible cybersecurity attacks and compromises. Covers continuous monitoring and adverse event analysis to identify anomalies and potential incidents.

ML1.summary: Basic continuous monitoring is enabled through Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and alert policies. Security signals are collected from endpoints and cloud services.

ML2.summary: Advanced detection capabilities analyze adverse events across the environment. Automated investigation, custom detection rules, and expanded alert tuning improve signal fidelity.

ML3.summary: Detection is comprehensive with advanced hunting, cross-workload correlation, and proactive threat intelligence. Monitoring covers the full attack lifecycle with minimal blind spots.

strategies.name: Respond (RS)
strategies.description: Take action regarding a detected cybersecurity incident. Covers incident management, incident analysis, incident response reporting and communication, and incident mitigation.

ML1.summary: Basic incident management processes are established. Defender for Endpoint provides initial incident triage, and foundational response playbooks guide containment actions.

ML2.summary: Incident analysis capabilities are expanded with automated investigation and remediation. Response workflows integrate across security tools for faster containment and communication.

ML3.summary: Incident response is mature with advanced mitigation, cross-functional coordination, and lessons-learned processes. Threat intelligence informs response actions and drives continuous improvement.

strategies.name: Recover (RC)
strategies.description: Restore assets and operations affected by a cybersecurity incident. Covers incident recovery plan execution and incident recovery communication to restore normal operations.

ML1.summary: Basic recovery plans and backup procedures are in place. SharePoint and OneDrive retention policies ensure critical data can be restored after an incident.

ML2.summary: Recovery processes are formalized with defined communication plans. Retention policies and site-level protections ensure data integrity and availability during recovery operations.

ML3.summary: Recovery capabilities are tested and validated regularly. Comprehensive site governance and sharing controls ensure resilient restoration with minimal data loss and business disruption.
